tagline: Thirty-three commits, zero releases, and the quiet hum of a kernel at work.
editionNote: A workmanlike week: @torvalds pushed 33 commits to linux with no releases, no PRs, and no fanfare.

articles.headline: 33 commits land in linux as @torvalds keeps the merge window turning
articles.deck: A steady week of kernel commits with no tagged releases or open pull requests to show for it.
articles.body: @torvalds pushed 33 commits to linux between March 16 and March 22. No new releases were tagged. No pull requests were opened or merged under the @torvalds account this week. The work speaks in diffs, not announcements — a quiet stretch that suggests either a merge window cooldown or upstream integration grinding along at its usual clip.
articles.illustrationPrompt: a single penguin steadily walking across a vast empty tundra under an overcast sky

closingNote: No releases. No PRs. Just commits. Some weeks the kernel simply breathes.
